skill.name: when-profiling-performance-use-performance-profiler
skill.description: Profiles application performance across CPU, memory, I/O, network, databases, and benchmarks, with optimization guidance.
skill.summary: Analyze bottlenecks, compare benchmarks, and plan performance fixes across application layers.
skill.author: Claude Code

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk hits, but the weak-cryptography detections are terminology false positives from performance examples that mention hash maps, cache hashes, and profiling metadata. The remaining findings reflect real operational risk: the skill includes an executable shell wrapper, npx-based coordination hooks, local filesystem profiling, network inspection patterns, and optional auto-apply optimization workflows. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Through npx Coordination Hooks
medium_findings.description: The shell wrapper and documentation invoke npx claude-flow@alpha hooks and memory commands. This is consistent with the skill design, but it executes an external package and can create supply-chain and environment side effects when run.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly present and would run if the slash wrapper is executed. The intent appears to be coordination, not malware, so confidence is high for operational risk but not malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Project Modification Workflow Can Apply Optimizations
medium_findings.description: The skill exposes apply and auto-apply optimization paths that can change a target project after profiling. Safety checks are described, but automatic code or configuration changes require user review and should not run on production code without backups.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files describe explicit apply and coder-agent flows. The shell script mostly simulates output, but the MCP and subagent instructions define behavior that can modify project files.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Read and Write Access for Profiling Outputs
medium_findings.description: The skill reads source files to estimate complexity and writes profiling artifacts such as CPU profiles, reports, and stream outputs. This is expected for profiling, but it can expose proprietary code or write large artifacts in the project tree.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are direct and contextually required for profiling. Risk depends on where the skill is run and what source files are included.

medium_findings.title: Network Profiling May Capture Request Metadata
medium_findings.description: The skill instruments HTTP requests and describes network request analysis. This can reveal endpoints, timings, and external API usage, so users should avoid capturing sensitive production traffic without authorization.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network profiling logic is clear, but no evidence shows credential exfiltration or hardcoded malicious endpoints. The risk is privacy and operational exposure from legitimate profiling.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak-cryptography findings appear to match words such as hash, hashmap, or cache-related text in performance documentation and schema examples. No evidence was found of MD5, SHA1, insecure crypto use, or password hashing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed nearby context shows performance and caching terminology rather than cryptographic algorithms. The static scanner likely matched generic hash-related words.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: A targeted search for suspicious override language found no instructions that attempted to bypass security review. The only matching term was a normal duration override option in the slash command help.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Search covered common prompt-injection phrases across the reviewed files. The single hit is benign command help text.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs Are Benign
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to the declared repository and documentation metadata, not to suspicious collection endpoints. No evidence found of data being sent to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are metadata fields at the end of the MCP descriptor. They are not used in request code in the reviewed context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runtime External Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The slash command conditionally runs npx claude-flow@alpha. Users should pin and trust external tooling before executing the wrapper.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wrapper directly contains npx invocations. The risk is supply-chain and execution side effects rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated Optimization Application
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can route generated optimizations to coder or apply workflows. Any generated patch should be reviewed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The apply workflow is explicitly documented. The exact implementation is not present, so the confidence is below very high.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Source File Inspection
dangerous_patterns.description: The profiler reads source files to extract functions and estimate complexity. This is useful, but it can expose private code to connected agents or memory hooks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly read files by path. Exposure depends on how the agent stores or transmits profiling results.

content.value_statement: Slow applications are hard to fix without clear measurements across code, memory, I/O, network, and databases. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through profiling, bottleneck analysis, optimization planning, and benchmark validation.
actual_capabilities: Profiles CPU, memory, I/O, network, and database performance using structured phases.
Identifies hot paths, memory leaks, slow queries, N+1 patterns, and slow external APIs.
Generates prioritized optimization recommendations with estimated impact and effort.
Supports quick, standard, deep, continuous, and stress-test profiling modes.
Compares baseline and optimized benchmark results for regression validation.
Coordinates profiling agents and optional Claude-Flow hooks for multi-step analysis.
limitations: Some provided workflows are templates or simulations and require working profilers in the target environment.
The slash command can run external npx hooks when executed on a machine with npx installed.
Auto-apply optimization workflows need human review before changing production code.
Network and filesystem profiling may expose sensitive local or production metadata.

use_cases.title: Debug a Slow API Release
use_cases.description: Measure latency, CPU hot paths, database queries, and request timing before deciding which bottleneck to fix first.

use_cases.title: Plan an Optimization Sprint
use_cases.description: Turn raw performance symptoms into ranked work items with impact, effort, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Validate Regression Risk
use_cases.description: Compare benchmark results against a baseline and flag throughput, latency, CPU, or memory regressions.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Bottleneck Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Profile this project for the main performance bottlenecks. Start with a quick scan of CPU, memory, I/O, and network behavior. Summarize the top three issues and the safest next measurement.

prompt_templates.title: Memory Leak Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate memory growth in this service. Compare heap behavior before and after workload execution, identify likely retained objects, and suggest cleanup changes with validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Database Query Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze database-related latency in this app. Look for slow queries, missing indexes, N+1 patterns, and connection pool issues. Rank recommendations by expected impact and implementation risk.

prompt_templates.title: Benchmark and Apply Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full performance optimization plan. Establish a baseline, identify bottlenecks, propose changes, define tests, run benchmark comparison, and list which changes are safe to apply only after review.

output_examples.input: Profile a Node.js API with high p95 latency.
output: Finds CPU hot paths in request processing and template rendering.
Reports slow database queries and one likely missing index.
Recommends caching, query indexing, and a benchmark plan before deployment.

output_examples.input: Check whether a recent optimization improved performance.
output: Compares throughput, average latency, p95 latency, CPU usage, and memory usage against the saved baseline.
Flags any regression above the configured tolerance.
Summarizes which changes should be kept, reviewed, or reverted.

output_examples.input: Investigate memory growth during a long workload.
output: Captures heap growth patterns and likely retention paths.
Highlights event listener, timer, and cache cleanup candidates.
Defines a repeatable validation run to confirm the leak is fixed.

best_practices: Run profiling in a controlled staging or development environment before production use.
Save a clean baseline before applying optimization recommendations.
Review all generated patches, migrations, and configuration changes before applying them.
anti_patterns: Do not run deep or continuous profiling on production systems without approval and monitoring.
Do not auto-apply generated optimizations without tests, backups, and code review.
Do not capture network or heap data that may contain secrets unless data handling is approved.

faq.question: What does this skill profile?
faq.answer: It covers CPU, memory, I/O, network, database behavior, benchmarks, and before-after performance comparisons.

faq.question: Can it apply fixes automatically?
faq.answer: It describes apply workflows, but changes should be reviewed before use. Treat generated optimizations as proposals.

faq.question: Does it require external tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Useful runs may need runtime profilers such as perf, Instruments, v8-profiler, heapdump, or database explain tools.

faq.question: Is it safe for production profiling?
faq.answer: Use production profiling only with approval. Deep profiling, network capture, and heap snapshots can affect performance or expose sensitive data.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows, including slash commands, subagents, and MCP-style tool definitions.

faq.question: What should users check before running it?
faq.answer: Confirm the project path, output directory, installed profilers, external npx package trust, and data handling rules.
